prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: many small winding rivers, each a different color and carrying a different kind of cargo (tiny floating numbers as pebbles, little gears, small clouds, musical ripples), all flowing together and merging into one calm shining sea where a friendly little boat sails confidently. Generous negative space, soft sky, Kurzgesagt and XKCD feel, one clear visual idea of many streams becoming one. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Many differently colored rivers carrying different cargo merge into a single shining sea where one small boat sails, showing diverse temporal problems uniting into one approach.

after_heading: 2. Everything Is Tokens: One Format for Six Modalities Intermediate
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a cheerful threading machine taking in wildly different objects (a tiny waveform, a small photo square, a little speech bubble, a temperature droplet, an event spark) and turning each into an identical round colored bead, then stringing all the beads onto one single long necklace thread. Expressive friendly machine face, clear before-and-after, generous negative space, XKCD and Kurzgesagt feel. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A friendly machine converts varied inputs like a waveform, photo, speech bubble, and temperature droplet into identical beads and strings them on one thread, showing six modalities reduced to one token format.
caption: Once everything becomes the same kind of bead on the same thread, a single model can read numbers, words, and images without ever changing its alphabet.

after_heading: 1. Why Multimodal: Decisions Fuse Numbers, Text, Images, and Events Beginner
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: several friendly little cooks each pouring a different ingredient into one shared bubbling pot, one pours a wiggly line of numbers, one pours flowing letters, one drops in a small picture, one tosses in a spark of an event, and the steam rising from the pot forms a single confident pointing arrow. Cozy kitchen warmth, expressive characters, generous negative space, Kurzgesagt and XKCD feel. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Several cooks pour different ingredients (numbers, letters, a picture, an event spark) into one shared pot whose steam forms a single pointing arrow, showing many modalities fused into one decision.
caption: A real decision is one broth simmered from many ingredients at once: the numbers, the news, the picture, and the events all go in the same pot before a single answer rises out.

after_heading: 1. The Convergence: Foundation Models + Agents + Sequences-as-Policies = Foundation Agents Beginner
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a single friendly versatile robot character standing confidently, sprouting helpful arms that each hold a different capability as a simple prop (a crystal ball for prediction, a tiny wrench for tools, a small memory spool, a steering wheel for acting), all the arms growing from one calm central body. Expressive face, balanced and not cluttered, generous negative space, XKCD and Kurzgesagt feel. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A single friendly robot sprouts arms holding a crystal ball, a wrench, a memory spool, and a steering wheel from one central body, showing prediction, tools, memory, and action converging into one foundation agent.
caption: The foundation agent is what happens when predicting, remembering, using tools, and acting stop being separate machines and become four arms of one body.

after_heading: 1. Scaling Laws: Model, Data, Compute, and the Power-Law Loss Curve Intermediate
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a determined little climber hauling an ever heavier backpack up a long curving slope that starts steep and then flattens into a nearly level plateau near the top, the climber sweating happily but each new step gaining far less height than the last. Clear smooth curve shape, expressive effort, generous negative space, Kurzgesagt and XKCD feel. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A climber with a growing backpack ascends a slope that starts steep then flattens to a plateau, each step rising less, showing diminishing returns of the power-law scaling curve.
caption: Scaling buys real progress, but the slope flattens: every doubling of effort lifts you a little less, which is why bigger is not automatically better.

after_heading: 1. The Synthesis: One Problem, One Toolkit, One Thread
prompt: A witty cartoon educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a single glowing thread held by a friendly hand at one end where a tiny modest old measuring gadget sits, the thread running rightward and growing brighter while small charming milestones bead along it (a little wave, a small recurring loop, a tiny attention starburst), ending at a confident capable robot reaching for the future, the whole thread forming one continuous unbroken line. Sense of a long journey, generous negative space, XKCD and Kurzgesagt feel. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: One continuous glowing thread runs from a small old measuring gadget through milestones like a wave, a loop, and an attention starburst to a confident robot reaching forward, showing the unbroken line from Kalman filter to foundation agent.
caption: From the humble recursive filter to the foundation agent runs one unbroken thread: the whole book has been the same idea of carrying the past forward, learned ever more powerfully.
